name: deep_sea_mining_plume_indigenous_fishing_grounds
premise: International Seabed Authority liaison Casey Young must recommend provisional approval for a polymetallic nodule collector whose sediment plume model disagrees with Indigenous Pacific fishers' traditional current knowledge. Biologist Ada Patel says vendor models undercount lateral drift toward tuna aggregation zones. Approving unlocks royalty streams for developing nations; denying delays climate-relevant mineral sourcing for grid batteries. Small island states split evenly on the council vote tonight.

agents.name: Casey Young
agents.identity: I am the ISA liaison translating science into provisional rules when vendor models conflict with Indigenous knowledge and geopolitical equity concerns.
agents.goal: I want minerals for decarbonization without repeating colonial harms on fishing rights that treaties nominally protect.

agents.name: Ada Patel
agents.identity: I am a fisheries biologist who ran independent plume particle tracking and who documented tuna catch declines in adjacent test squares.
agents.goal: I want provisional approval withheld until co-designed monitoring buoys are deployed with veto triggers for fishers, even if that delays mining revenue.

choices: I will recommend provisional approval with mandatory fishers' veto triggers on turbidity thresholds and revenue-sharing escrow, accepting that miners may walk from thin margins.
I will recommend denial until independent replication of plume models completes under joint authorship with Indigenous observers, accepting battery mineral timeline delays globally.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Council president gave Casey Young the floor for a binding recommendation before midnight UTC. Casey Young must choose between conditional approval with vetoes or denial pending replication. What does Casey Young do?
